title: SCHWARTZ HYDROZIRCONATION
summary: Overview page for Schwartz hydrozirconation describing the use of Cp2ZrHCl to hydrozirconate alkenes and alkynes, giving alkyl- and alkenylzirconium intermediates that can be trapped with electrophiles, carbon monoxide, or transmetalation partners.
notes: Key concepts: rapid syn hydrozirconation, strong terminal-alkene preference, facile isomerization of polyenes under controlled conditions, and broad utility of organozirconium intermediates for trapping or cross-coupling chemistry.

title: SCHWARTZ HYDROZIRCONATION
summary: Application page for Schwartz hydrozirconation featuring Curacin A, Apoptolidin, and the pyrrolidinone core of isodomoic acid G, illustrating chemoselective hydrozirconation of polyenes and alkynes followed by electrophile trapping or nickel-catalyzed bond construction in complex-target synthesis.
notes: Examples emphasize one-carbon homologation of conjugated trienes, selective terminal hydrozirconation of internal/terminal alkynes, and tandem hydrozirconation/transmetalation sequences that install densely functionalized diene side chains.

title: SEYFERTH-GILBERT HOMOLOGATION
summary: Overview page for the Seyferth-Gilbert homologation describing aldehyde- and ketone-to-alkyne conversion with diazophosphonate reagents, including the Ohira-Bestmann modification for especially mild preparation of terminal alkynes from aldehydes.
notes: Key concepts: carbonyl homologation through diazo-phosphonate addition and rearrangement, formation of internal or terminal alkynes depending on reagent class, and room-temperature Bestmann-Ohira conditions that tolerate sensitive functionality.

title: SEYFERTH-GILBERT HOMOLOGATION
summary: Application page for the Seyferth-Gilbert homologation featuring Polycavernoside A, (-)-Minquartynoic acid, the C5-C20 subunit of the aplyronines, and (-)-Laulimalide, highlighting strategic aldehyde-to-alkyne homologation in marine natural-product synthesis.
notes: Examples emphasize terminal-alkyne installation on highly oxygenated fragments, use of the Ohira-Bestmann protocol on fragile aldehydes, and deployment of the resulting alkynes in halovinylation, macrocyclization, or further coupling chemistry.

title: SHARPLESS ASYMMETRIC AMINOHYDROXYLATION
summary: Overview page for Sharpless asymmetric aminohydroxylation describing osmium-catalyzed enantioselective cis-aminohydroxylation of alkenes with chloramine-derived nitrogen sources to access protected vicinal amino alcohols.
notes: Key concepts: catalytic osmium chemistry with chiral ligands related to SAD/SAE systems, use of N-halo carbamates or chloramines as nitrogen donors, formation of enantiopure cis amino alcohols, and ligand-controlled regioselectivity in electron-deficient alkenes.

title: SHARPLESS ASYMMETRIC AMINOHYDROXYLATION
summary: Application page for Sharpless asymmetric aminohydroxylation featuring Ustiloxin D, Dragmacidin A, the G- and F-ring phenylglycine precursors of teicoplanin aglycone, and (-)-Ephedradine A, demonstrating enantioselective amino alcohol installation on cinnamates, vinyl indoles, and styrene derivatives.
notes: Examples emphasize pseudoeenantiomeric ligand choice, N-Boc or N-Cbz protected amino alcohol formation, high enantioselectivity on halogenated styrenes and vinyl indoles, and downstream manipulation of the β-amino alcohol functionality in complex targets.

title: SHARPLESS ASYMMETRIC DIHYDROXYLATION
summary: Overview page for Sharpless asymmetric dihydroxylation describing catalytic osmium-mediated conversion of alkenes into enantiopure cis vicinal diols with AD-mix α or β and Cinchona-derived ligands.
notes: Key concepts: AD-mix α/β reagent sets, empirical mnemonic for face selection, ligand-accelerated catalysis, broad alkene scope, and cyclic osmate ester formation followed by oxidative turnover to deliver syn dihydroxylation products.

title: SHARPLESS ASYMMETRIC DIHYDROXYLATION
summary: Application page for Sharpless asymmetric dihydroxylation featuring (+)-Zaragozic acid C, KDO, and (+)-1-Epiaustraline, showing stereodefined installation of densely oxygenated vicinal-diol arrays in carbohydrate and alkaloid synthesis.
notes: Examples emphasize sequential SAD operations on dienes, challenging face selectivity in oxygen heterocycles, and extensive ligand screening to favor the desired diastereomer in tandem cycloaddition-derived frameworks.

title: SHARPLESS ASYMMETRIC EPOXIDATION
summary: Overview page for Sharpless asymmetric epoxidation describing titanium-tartrate-catalyzed enantioselective epoxidation of allylic alcohols with tert-butyl hydroperoxide to give epoxy alcohols of predictable absolute configuration.
notes: Key concepts: Ti(O-iPr)4/DET/TBHP catalyst system, predictable tartrate-controlled facial selectivity, kinetic resolution of racemic allylic alcohols, and crucial influence of allylic alcohol geometry and molecular sieves on high ee.

title: SHARPLESS ASYMMETRIC EPOXIDATION
summary: Application page for Sharpless asymmetric epoxidation featuring (+)-Parviflorin, (20R)-Homocamptothecin, (-)-Laulimalide, and (+)-/- Madindoline A/B, illustrating late-stage enantioselective epoxidation of allylic alcohols embedded in highly functionalized natural-product frameworks.
notes: Examples emphasize regioselective bis-allylic alcohol epoxidation, use of L-(+)-DET versus D-(-)-DET to set C20 stereochemistry, and selective oxidation within macrocyclic polyol systems where only one allylic alcohol is addressed.
